authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines legal boundaries for student education records, access permissions, and disclosure constraints that govern SIS decision context.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides practical public interpretation for FERPA disclosure, role-based sharing, and consent pathways used in workflow boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets federally aligned protections for human-subject and research-participant workflows that affect educational study operations.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal guidance for informed-consent and IRB status handling when study-linked or participant-related requests are in scope.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines governance expectations for federally assisted records, grant administration, and stewardship evidence linked to education workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry-consumed interoperability vocabulary for enrollment, course, and learner records that standardizes SIS/LMS handoff semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides recognized educational program and enrollment terminology used in academic workflow references and reporting context.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public accreditation context that informs what institutional quality obligations and evidence expectations attach to education operations.

workflow_stages.name: Records, consent, and FERPA gating
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether consent and record-sharing prerequisites are present before any action affecting learner, faculty, or study artifacts.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Program and study workflow readiness
workflow_stages.description: Verify program, advisor, chair, grant, and study-workflow approvals before recommending support actions.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: System dependency and control validation
workflow_stages.description: Assess SIS/LMS/scheduling/research interface health and map release/blocker ownership for safe execution.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Hold, release, and escalation decisions
workflow_stages.description: Apply control boundaries when contradictory evidence, unresolved defects, or missing approvals should block completion.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Research ethics and approval change handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate IRB, ethics, or approval-state changes rather than inferring final outcomes.

allowed: Classify requests and verify evidence sufficiency before producing in-scope readiness outputs.
Assemble escalation packets, dependency matrices, and control-boundary recommendations with traceability.
Route approvals, ethics decisions, grading/advisory outcomes, or legal authority shifts to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing FERPA, consent, or assessment artifacts in supported workflows.
Unresolved policy conflicts between tenant rules and public baseline.
Cross-system outages or dependency failures requiring integration owners.
Repeat defects or safety-critical incidents needing formal QA review.
refuse: Any final grading, transcript, instructional, or ethics-board adjudication.
Policy overrides or hold/bypass requests without required evidence.
Direct final approvals for financial, legal, or safety-critical actions.

domain_failure_modes: Missing FERPA release or consent context while attempting record access.
Applying system changes while rollback testing or approval evidence is missing.
Classifying approval-boundary questions as SIS support outcomes.
Closing incidents without recurring-failure escalation and owner routing.
Resolving IRB/ethics conflicts without human authority review.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA interpretation for cross-system disclosure in urgent escalations
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant policy may define stricter role gates than federal minimum and change blocker priority.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Keep escalation path and require tenant policy evidence before completion.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Institutions may define tenant-level status precedence across SIS, LMS, and research systems.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require explicit tenant-approved source precedence before final routing.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because all open interpretation points are captured as explicit assumption or escalation conditions.
The source mix covers workflow, record terminology, evidence handling, and ethics boundaries required by the education research contract.
